summary: Broadcom delivered record FQ2 2026 revenue of $22.19B (+48% YoY), narrowly missing $22.27B consensus. AI semiconductor revenue surged 143% YoY to $10.8B — 49% of total revenue. Non-GAAP EPS $2.44 beat $2.40 est. Infrastructure software missed at $7.18B vs $7.32B expected. Q3 guidance $29.4B (+84% YoY) beat $28.53B consensus. Stock fell ~14% AH as Hock Tan held the $56B FY2026 and $100B FY2027 AI revenue targets.
execSummary: Broadcom delivered record FQ2 2026 revenue, EBITDA (69% margin), and FCF ($10.26B). AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY) hit a new high. The -14% AH sell-off: (1) software miss $7.18B vs $7.32B, (2) AI target not raised from $56B, (3) stock at all-time highs +40% YTD. Q3 guide $29.4B (+84% YoY) beat consensus. Rating: BUY · PT $550.
revenueGrowthAnalysis: AI semiconductor revenue ($10.8B, +143% YoY) drove the quarter — sequential acceleration from $8.4B in FQ1. Custom ASIC demand from hyperscalers continues to scale. Non-AI semis ($4.2B, +6%) showed early recovery. Infrastructure software (+9%, ARR +17%) missed whisper expectations by $140M.
segmentAnalysis: Semiconductor Solutions (68%): $15.0B +79% YoY. AI semis $10.8B +143%, Non-AI $4.2B +6%, Gross margin ~70%, Op margin ~62% (+460bps). Infrastructure Software (32%): $7.18B +9% YoY, ARR +17%, Gross margin 93%, Op margin 79% (+310bps). Software MISSED $7.32B consensus by $140M.
industryAnalysis: AI ASIC/networking TAM estimated $150-200B+ by 2027-2028 at 40-60% CAGR. Hyperscaler AI capex accelerating. Broadcom and Marvell are the two primary custom AI silicon vendors. NVIDIA Spectrum-X is the key competitive threat to Broadcom AI networking ASICs.
guidanceAnalysis: Q3 FY2026: Revenue ~$29.4B (+84% YoY) vs consensus $28.53B — beat $870M. AI semiconductor ~$16.0B (+200%+ YoY). Non-GAAP op margin ~67%. FY2026 AI: $56B reaffirmed (not raised). FY2027 AI: >$100B reaffirmed. Q3 AI guide implies $64B annualised run-rate.
mgmtPositive: Hock Tan: Record revenue, operating profit and free cash flow — AI semiconductor revenue accelerating
Q3 AI semiconductor guide $16B (+200%+ YoY) — acceleration continues, not plateauing
FCF $10.26B at 46% margin in a single quarter — best-in-class capital generation
VMware ARR +17% YoY — subscription migration on track with 93% software gross margins
EBITDA margin 69% expanding (+100bps QoQ) — operating leverage confirmed at scale
mgmtNegative: Infrastructure software $7.18B missed $7.32B consensus — enterprise deal elongation acknowledged
FY2026 AI target $56B and FY2027 >$100B held, not raised — disappointed investors
Customer concentration: 2-3 hyperscalers drive majority of AI revenue — binary risk
Non-AI semiconductor recovery (+6%) remains below peak
bullPoints: AI ASIC secular compounder: >$100B FY2027 AI revenue backed by multi-year take-or-pay contracts
FCF machine: $10.26B in one quarter (46% margin) — $30-40B/year capital return capacity
Margin expansion: AI semis ~72% gross margin scaling as AI mix rises toward 70% of semis
VMware compounding: 93% gross margins, ARR +17% — de-risks against semiconductor cycle
bearPoints: Hyperscaler concentration: 2-3 customers drive AI revenue — capex pullback is a binary risk
Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia represent multi-year ASIC in-sourcing threats
NVIDIA Spectrum-X networking threatens Broadcom high-margin AI networking ASIC franchise
Valuation: ~42x FY2026 non-GAAP EPS at $415 — priced for flawless execution
nearRisks: Whisper-number treadmill: any AI shortfall vs $16B Q3 guide triggers sharp re-rating
Two consecutive software misses would call VMware ARR growth story into question
Macro / enterprise IT spend caution elongating deal cycles
longRisks: Hyperscaler ASIC in-sourcing: Google, Meta, Amazon investing in proprietary silicon over 3-5 years
NVIDIA Spectrum-X: integrated GPU+NIC+switch could displace Broadcom AI networking ASICs
U.S. export controls expansion could limit non-AI semiconductor revenue in China
valuation: At ~$415 (post-AH): Market cap ~$1.70T. Forward P/E FY2026: ~42x. Forward P/E FY2027: ~26x. EV/EBITDA: ~29x. PEG ~0.55x on 76% FY2026 EPS growth. Analyst consensus: Strong Buy, avg PT $497-$507. Jefferies $550, Deutsche Bank $515.
ptBullCase: $720. FY2027 non-GAAP EPS ~$17.20 (AI $120B+, software +12%). 42x forward = $722. Probability: 20%.
ptBearCase: $310. FY2027 non-GAAP EPS ~$12.50 (AI $75B, software flat). 25x = $313. Probability: 25%.
thesis: Broadcom is a compelling AI compounder — custom ASIC moat backed by multi-year hyperscaler contracts, 93% gross margin software, and $10B+ FCF/quarter. The -14% AH drop is a whisper-number overreaction. BUY · PT $550.
